article_meta.title: After some 150 students walked out of Redlands schools early this month in support of immigrants, they were dealt an une

reasoning: The summary maintains a neutral tone, attributes viewpoints appropriately, and avoids loaded language from the source. Minor interpretive phrasing appears (e.g., "more supportively or leniently"), but overall it does not adopt the article’s framing as fact.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; slight interpretive phrasing ("supportively or leniently").
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Legal context attributed to ACLU and CA law; minor generalization to "legal experts" not directly quoted.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative claims are attributed to students or officials; no subjective judgments stated as fact.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about unobserved motives; views are attributed.
custom_reasoning.slant: Both district and student perspectives presented; slight emphasis on contrast with other districts.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits some peripheral perspectives present in the article (e.g., out-of-state officials, counterprotester, law enforcement).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal; "supportively or leniently" is mildly subjective but contextually grounded.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced representation of discipline rationale and rights context.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No undue deference to any source type.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Does not inherit charged framing; uses careful attributions.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers main perspectives (district, students, ACLU/law, parents) but not all minor ones mentioned.
